content.problemStatement:
There is a hostel with MMM rooms arranged in a **circular clockwise** order as shown below:



Each room can accommodate any number of students.

NNN students are allotted rooms in the hostel such that the ithi^{th}ith student is initially allotted room AiA_iAi‚Äã.

If the ithi^{th}ith student stays in the jthj^{th}jth room, then his happiness will be Hi,jH_{i,j}Hi,j‚Äã.

We define the total happiness of the hostel as the **sum** of happiness of all students after they are reallocated the rooms.

Given an integer XXX, you are allowed to do the following operation:


Choose two different students iii and jjj;
Shift the ithi^{th}ith student by XXX rooms in the clockwise direction and the jthj^{th}jth student by XXX rooms in the anticlockwise direction.
Find the **maximum** total happiness of the hostel after any (possibly zero) number of operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM, and XXX ‚Äî the number of students, number of rooms in hostel and shift possible in one operation.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã,  the room allotted to ithi^{th}ith student initially.
The next NNN lines contains MMM integers each, the ithi^{th}ith of which describes the happiness value of ithi^{th}ith student for every room 1‚â§j‚â§M1 \leq j \leq M1‚â§j‚â§M.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** total happiness of the hostel after any (possibly zero) number of operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN and MMM over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: As there is only 111 student, we cannot choose two different students to apply any operation and thus maximum happiness is H1,1=5H_{1,1} = 5H1,1‚Äã=5.
Test case 2: We will apply one operation on i=3i = 3i=3 and j=2j = 2j=2. Thus, the reallocated rooms are room 111 for student 111 and 222 and room 222 for student 333. The happiness values is H1,1+H2,1+H3,2=1+2+2=5H_{1,1} + H_{2,1} + H_{3,2} = 1 + 2 + 2 = 5H1,1‚Äã+H2,1‚Äã+H3,2‚Äã=1+2+2=5.
It can be shown that this is the maximum total happiness that can be achieved using any number of operations.
Test case 3: Any operation you choose will decrease the current happiness of the hostel. Thus maximum happiness is H1,1+H2,2+H3,1=1+2+2=5H_{1,1} + H_{2,2} + H_{3,1} = 1+2+2 = 5H1,1‚Äã+H2,2‚Äã+H3,1‚Äã=1+2+2=5.
